page: Microsoft Azure FX4mds is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the FXMDVS server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. It is configured with 84.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a high resource density of 21.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

For storage, the server is equipped with 172 GB of local disk storage and is premium storage capable, enabling high-performance storage attachments. The hardware configuration does not include dedicated GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The FX4mds offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring large memory capacities without the need to scale up to higher vCPU tiers. This resource balance is suited for hosting relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise applications with substantial memory requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure FX4mds is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the FXMDVS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 21.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 172 GB of local disk storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for memory-intensive workloads, the FX4mds provides high memory density relative to its compute capacity. This hardware profile is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without requiring a high number of processing cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4mds is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 172 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring high RAM-to-core density and local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
